Politics.headline: U.S.-Iran talks pause for now, disagreements remain
Politics.detail: Negotiations between the United States and Iran in Pakistan to end the six-week war have paused, with technical experts from both sides set to exchange documents after 14 hours of talks; the outcome could determine the fate of a fragile ceasefire and the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

Politics.headline: Mauritius vows to reclaim Chagos sovereignty, says FM
Politics.detail: Mauritius will spare no effort to reclaim sovereignty over the Chagos Archipelago, according to Foreign Minister Dhananjay Ramful, who spoke at the Indian Ocean Conference; this comes after the British government shelved legislation to transfer sovereignty after the United States withdrew its backing.

Politics.headline: Crown Prince of Abu Dhabi to begin official visit to China on Sunday
Politics.detail: Sheikh Khaled bin Mohamed bin Zayed Al Nahyan, Crown Prince of Abu Dhabi, is commencing an official visit to China on April 12, 2026, to strengthen bilateral relations and expand cooperation, accompanied by a high-level delegation.

Politics.headline: Xi tells Taiwan opposition leader reunification is 'certainty of history' in rare Beijing meeting
Politics.detail: Chinese President Xi Jinping met with a Taiwan opposition leader in Beijing, stating that reunification is a 'certainty of history'.

Politics.headline: Carney rallies Liberal supporters ahead of crucial byelections that could solidify his power
Politics.detail: Prime Minister Mark Carney is using a message of national unity to rally supporters ahead of byelections that could secure a Liberal majority government.

Finance & Economy.headline: US Inflation Rises Amid Hormuz Blockade, Consumer Sentiment Plunges
Finance & Economy.detail: Inflation in the US has increased due to the Strait of Hormuz blockade, while consumer sentiment has significantly decreased. The University of Michigan's Consumer Sentiment Index plummeted to a historic low, underscoring the Iran conflict’s immediate impact on confidence.

Finance & Economy.headline: Ray Dalio's 'War Thesis' Flags Dollar Debasement Against Bitcoin
Finance & Economy.detail: Ray Dalio's economic war thesis highlights dollar debasement as a key factor for Bitcoin's performance. His data links weak money to Bitcoin outperformance, with Bitcoin at $73,446.

Finance & Economy.headline: How an 18.2% CAGR is reshaping the fintech industry through to 2034
Finance & Economy.detail: The global fintech market reached $460.76 billion in 2026 and is projected to reach $1.76 trillion by 2034, implying an 18.2% compound annual growth rate. This growth is driven by technology adoption and attracts significant capital inflows.

Finance & Economy.headline: Gap Between Rich and Poor Nations Is Growing Even Wider, UN Report Says
Finance & Economy.detail: A UN report concludes that the gap between rich and poor nations is widening, with unfulfilled promises to overhaul global financial institutions. Geopolitical tensions, rising trade barriers, and climate-related shocks are compounding the struggles of developing countries.

Finance & Economy.headline: Oil and gas crisis from Iran war worse than 1973, ​1979 and 2022 together, says IEA
Finance & Economy.detail: The ongoing conflict with Iran has created an oil and gas crisis that the IEA says is more severe than previous crises. This crisis is reflected in rising energy costs and increased inflation.

Technology.headline: Police arrest a suspect in a Molotov attack at OpenAI CEO's San Francisco home
Technology.detail: A suspect has been arrested in connection with a Molotov cocktail attack at the San Francisco home of OpenAI's CEO, highlighting increasing concerns around the safety and security of tech executives.

Technology.headline: Google Rolls Out E2EE Encryption for iOS and Android
Technology.detail: Gmail users can now send encrypted messages on iOS and Android, but it's currently limited to Workspace users with an Enterprise Plus subscription and specific add-ons where admins have enabled the feature.

Technology.headline: Scientists make world's smallest QR code in data storage breakthrough
Technology.detail: Scientists have achieved a data storage breakthrough by creating the world's smallest QR code, potentially revolutionizing data density and storage capabilities.

Technology.headline: Meta, CoreWeave deepen AI cloud partnership with fresh $21 billion deal
Technology.detail: Meta and CoreWeave are expanding their AI cloud partnership with a new $21 billion deal, as demand for AI infrastructure continues to surge.

Technology.headline: The $30 Google TV stick may be the budget Chromecast successor we've been waiting for
Technology.detail: A new, budget-friendly Google TV stick priced at $30 is potentially on the horizon, offering an affordable option for users seeking a Chromecast alternative.

Science.headline: Experimental Drug Can Reverse Osteoarthritis in Weeks, Animal Research Shows
Science.detail: A new experimental drug has shown promise in reversing osteoarthritis in animal studies within weeks, offering hope for a potential treatment for this debilitating joint condition.

Science.headline: The Universe's Most Pristine Ancient Star Is Surprisingly Close
Science.detail: Scientists have discovered the most pristine ancient star found to date, located surprisingly close to Earth, offering a unique opportunity to study the early universe.

Science.headline: Coffee's sweet spot may help mental health in the long run
Science.detail: Recent studies suggest that moderate coffee consumption may have long-term benefits for mental health.

Science.headline: New biological marker of early-stage Alzheimer's disease uncovered
Science.detail: Scientists have identified a new biological marker that could help in the early detection of Alzheimer's disease.

Science.headline: This Week in Science: Artemis II Returns, a Chimp 'Civil War', And More!
Science.detail: This week's science news includes the return of Artemis II, reports on chimp 'civil war', and other fascinating discoveries.

Health.headline: To fix a patient's irregular heartbeat, doctors first tested its digital 'twin'
Health.detail: Doctors are now using digital twins to test treatments for irregular heartbeats before applying them to the actual patient, a pioneering move in personalized medicine.

Health.headline: FDA grants speedy approval to Eli Lilly's weight-loss pill for obesity
Health.detail: The FDA has granted speedy approval to Eli Lilly's new weight-loss pill, offering a new option for individuals struggling with obesity.

Health.headline: Too young for the MMR shot, babies become 'sitting ducks' in measles outbreaks
Health.detail: Babies who are too young to receive the MMR vaccine are highly vulnerable during measles outbreaks, such as the one in South Carolina.

Health.headline: Putting on weight earlier in life may be more dangerous than previously thought
Health.detail: Research indicates that gaining weight in early adulthood significantly elevates the risk of premature death, especially from major diseases like heart disease and diabetes, suggesting early adulthood obesity is more dangerous than previously believed.

Health.headline: People who fast longer overnight and start their day with an early breakfast were more likely to have a lower BMI years later
Health.detail: A new study suggests that people who extend their overnight fast and eat breakfast earlier are more likely to have a lower BMI over the long term, possibly due to the impact on metabolic processes.

Sports.headline: Alcaraz and Sinner to face off in Monte Carlo final
Sports.detail: Carlos Alcaraz and Jannik Sinner, the world's top two players, will compete in the Monte Carlo Masters final, with the winner claiming the World No. 1 ranking.

Sports.headline: Arsenal suffer major blow as Liverpool earn vital win
Sports.detail: Arsenal's defeat by Bournemouth has opened the Premier League title race, while Liverpool's victory over Fulham has boosted their chances of Champions League qualification.

Sports.headline: Chou Tien-chen reaches first Asia Championships semifinals in 7 years
Sports.detail: Taiwan's Chou Tien-chen advanced to the Badminton Asia Championships semifinals after defeating Singapore's Loh Kean Yew.

Sports.headline: Tyson Fury beats Arslanbek Makhmudov by unanimous decision
Sports.detail: Tyson Fury defeated Arslanbek Makhmudov by unanimous decision in his boxing return and wants to fight Joshua next.

Sports.headline: Samson Shines With Ton As CSK Beat Delhi Capitals For First Win Of IPL 2026
Sports.detail: Samson scored a century as CSK defeated Delhi Capitals to record their first win of IPL 2026. Samson also broke Dhoni's record with his first ton for CSK.

Entertainment.headline: Asha Bhosle Has Not Suffered A Cardiac Arrest, Granddaughter Shares Latest Health Update Of Singer
Entertainment.detail: Bollywood singer Asha Bhosle was admitted to Mumbai’s Breach Candy hospital due to extreme exhaustion and a chest infection, according to her granddaughter, Zanai Bhosle, refuting earlier reports of a cardiac arrest. Zanai shared that her grandmother is receiving treatment and requested privacy.

Entertainment.headline: Grab driver suspended for lewd Facebook post about actress
Entertainment.detail: A Grab driver in the Philippines has been suspended after posting a selfie with actress Xyriel Manabat on Facebook, accompanied by lewd remarks about her body. The Land Transportation Franchising and Regulatory Board (LTFRB) issued a show cause order and preventive suspension.

Entertainment.headline: BINI delivers electrifying performance at Coachella
Entertainment.detail: BINI, the first all-Filipino group to perform at Coachella, delivered a high-energy 45-minute set on the Mojave stage, featuring Tagalog songs, cultural fashion, and choreography rooted in Filipino identity. The group opened in gold “Warrior” outfits designed by Marian Zara, with custom details by Job Dacon.

Entertainment.headline: Ryan Gosling describes his astro-nerd look in 'Project Hail Mary'
Entertainment.detail: Ryan Gosling, the star of "Project Hail Mary," opening Friday in U.S. theatres, worked with directors Phil Lord and Christopher Miller, writer Drew Goddard and book author Andy Weir, and credits his kids and Val Kilmer for inspiring his character's stylish astronaut look in the sci-fi film, where he plays a science teacher on a mission to save Earth.

Entertainment.headline: Afrika Bambaataa, hip-hop pioneer, dies at 68
Entertainment.detail: Hip-hop pioneer Afrika Bambaataa has passed away at the age of 68. Bambaataa was a highly influential figure in the development of hip-hop music and culture.

Crime & Justice.headline: New York subway stabbings leave 3 hurt as police shoot and kill knife-wielding man, officials say
Crime & Justice.detail: A knife-wielding man was shot and killed by police after he stabbed three people in a New York subway station.

Crime & Justice.headline: Machete-wielding man killed by police in New York's Grand Central station
Crime & Justice.detail: Three elderly victims were wounded at the transit hub attack as authorities continue to investigate.

Crime & Justice.headline: London police arrest more than 200 at protest backing banned group Palestine Action
Crime & Justice.detail: London police have arrested more than 200 people during a protest against the ban on the group Palestine Action.

Crime & Justice.headline: Suspect linked to University of Ottawa lockdown found with replica firearm: police
Crime & Justice.detail: The accused has been charged with possession of a weapon dangerous to the public peace, as well as mischief — preventing the lawful enjoyment of property.

Crime & Justice.headline: Cowboys defensive back Markquese Bell is arrested on drug possession charges near Dallas.
Crime & Justice.detail: Cowboys defensive back Markquese Bell is arrested on drug possession charges near Dallas.

Education.headline: Strengthening polytechnics
Education.detail: Polytechnics are gaining greater autonomy to offer bachelor's, master's, and doctoral programs in high-skill fields like AI and semiconductors, as part of a move to elevate their status as primary pathways for higher education. The Higher Education Ministry is planning comprehensive reforms, including a new Polytechnic Act, with aims to produce one million graduates by 2027.

Education.headline: Government launches new research funding to strengthen school food programs across Canada
Education.detail: The Canadian government has launched new research funding aimed at strengthening school food programs across the country. This initiative aims to improve the nutritional quality and accessibility of food provided in schools.

Education.headline: AUArts Announces New Bachelor of Creative Industries Degree Linking Art, Craft, Design, Industry and Technology
Education.detail: Alberta University of the Arts (AUArts) has announced a new Bachelor of Creative Industries degree that links art, craft, design, industry, and technology. This program is designed to prepare students for careers in the creative sector by integrating various disciplines.

Education.headline: Algoma University Researchers Publish Groundbreaking Study in Leading Scientific Journal
Education.detail: Researchers at Algoma University have published a groundbreaking study in a leading scientific journal. The study's findings are expected to contribute significantly to their field.

Education.headline: Kinder Ready: Adaptive Learning for Personalized Progress Tracking
Education.detail: Kinder Ready has introduced adaptive learning technology to support personalized student progress tracking in early childhood education, enabling educators to assess and guide young learners towards kindergarten readiness. The platform uses data to recommend customized academic pathways and enhance collaboration between home and classroom.

Lifestyle.headline: Food delivery drones take flight in the Northeast
Lifestyle.detail: A new service is bringing food delivery drones to the Northeast, offering a potentially faster and more convenient way to receive meals.

Lifestyle.headline: Rubina Dilaik Busts Weight-Loss Myth: "Skipping Meals Has Not Helped Anyone"
Lifestyle.detail: Actress Rubina Dilaik shares her insights on weight loss, emphasizing that skipping meals is not an effective strategy.

Lifestyle.headline: 'Casual without being sloppy': why flannel shirts are making a comeback now
Lifestyle.detail: Flannel shirts are experiencing a resurgence in popularity as a versatile and comfortable fashion choice.

Lifestyle.headline: Women reveal surprising effects of being bald: 'It's a luxury'
Lifestyle.detail: Women discuss the unexpected benefits and liberating feeling of embracing baldness.

Lifestyle.headline: Top 10 travel trends in 2026: From AI powered home to litmus test trips
Lifestyle.detail: A look at the emerging trends shaping the travel industry in 2026, including AI-powered home rentals and unique 'litmus test' travel experiences.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: EDITORIAL: Cheng ratified Xi's view of Taiwan
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Chinese Nationalist Party (KMT) Chairwoman Cheng Li-wun met with Chinese President Xi Jinping in Beijing, framing her visit as historic and emphasizing the history of relations, which some see as ratifying Xi's view of Taiwan.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Decency, civility and public order
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Philippine Interior Secretary Jonvic Remulla's directive to prohibit disorderly acts is examined through the lens of public administration, arguing that enforcing laws against minor offenses is crucial for maintaining public order and preventing societal breakdown.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Mythos AI alarm bells: Fair warning or trick?
Opinion/Editorial.detail: Anthropic's postponement of releasing its new AI model, Claude Mythos, which is highly skilled at coding, has sparked both alarm and skepticism, with some seeing it as a potential tool for hackers and others viewing it as a marketing tactic.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Remembering C D Gopinath, the last link with the first Test victory
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An Indian Express opinion piece reflects on the legacy of C D Gopinath.

Opinion/Editorial.headline: Elastic rules: On the Plastic Waste Management (Amendment) Rules, 2026.
Opinion/Editorial.detail: An editorial in The Hindu discusses the Plastic Waste Management (Amendment) Rules, 2026.

World.headline: Direct U.S.-Iran talks on ending war stretch well past midnight in Islamabad
World.detail: Vice President JD Vance is leading the highest level of face-to-face engagement between leaders of the United States and Iran in decades in Islamabad, Pakistan, as they attempt to find a resolution to the ongoing conflict.

World.headline: Historic Iran-US talks to continue for a second day; Israel pounds Lebanon
World.detail: Despite ongoing conflict, the United States and Iran are continuing historic talks for a second day, while Israel continues its strikes on Lebanon.

World.headline: Pakistan sends fighter jets to Saudi Arabia amid fragile US-Iran ceasefire
World.detail: Amidst a delicate ceasefire between the US and Iran, Pakistan has dispatched fighter jets to Saudi Arabia, signaling a complex geopolitical landscape.

World.headline: Russia-Ukraine Orthodox Easter ceasefire begins
World.detail: A ceasefire has commenced between Russia and Ukraine to observe Orthodox Easter, offering a temporary respite from the ongoing conflict.

World.headline: Peru holds presidential election amid a decade of political tumult
World.detail: Peru is holding a presidential election, navigating through a period marked by significant political instability and change.

Zimbabwe.headline: Beitbridge Border Post transformation drives trade efficiency
Zimbabwe.detail: The country's Beitbridge Border Post has emerged as a game-changer in the region following a major upgrade that has improved trade efficiency.

Zimbabwe.headline: Mono-currency transition will be seamless, risk-free: RBZ
Zimbabwe.detail: The Reserve Bank of Zimbabwe (RBZ) has assured the public that the transition to a mono-currency system will be seamless and without risk.

Zimbabwe.headline: Armed robbers steal police rifle in Harare ambush
Zimbabwe.detail: Armed robbers ambushed a police patrol in Harare, overpowering three officers and stealing a service rifle, a vehicle, and mobile phones.

Zimbabwe.headline: Mnangagwa fires CIO boss Mangwanya, names diplomat Chikawa as new spy chief
Zimbabwe.detail: President Emmerson Mnangagwa has dismissed Central Intelligence Organisation (CIO) Director-General Fulton Mangwanya and appointed a veteran diplomat, Chikawa, as the new spy chief, though no explanation was offered for the abrupt decision.

Zimbabwe.headline: Spike in unlicensed dog breeding in Zimbabwe threatens public safety and health
Zimbabwe.detail: Unlicensed dog breeding in Zimbabwe is surging, leading to dangerous dog attacks and public health concerns, as enforcement of breeding regulations remains weak, particularly in urban centers. Dog bite cases have become a pressing public health concern.
